paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. IV. Consecutive holes
summary: The informal question of whether every (2,φ)-controlled triangle-free graph with sufficiently large chromatic number must contain a 4-hole remains open. Scott and Seymour proved Statement 2.1 for all ℓ ≥ 5 in the source paper, explicitly noting they could not decide the ℓ = 4 case. A thorough web search found no subsequent paper resolving this question, and the sole internal-corpus candidate (arXiv:2306.02364) is about tournament structure and is unrelated.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is 'Some results and problems on tournament structure' by Nguyen, Scott, Seymour. Its abstract concerns subtournaments with large chromatic number and domination number in tournaments; it makes no mention of 4-holes, (2,φ)-controlled graphs, or the ℓ=4 question from arXiv:1509.06563. The fuzz-67 match is a false positive.

search_queries: Scott Seymour "controlled" "triangle-free" "4-hole" chromatic number induced subgraphs
arxiv 1509.06563 Scott Seymour consecutive holes 4-hole conjecture follow-up
"triangle-free" "4-hole" chromatic number "controlled" graph conjecture Scott Seymour 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: The conjecture is phrased as an informal question in the paper, not a numbered conjecture. Statement 2.1 (for ℓ ≥ 5) is fully proved; the ℓ = 4 case is singled out as the remaining open instance. A 2021 paper by Davies (arXiv:2104.05907) disproves a different Scott–Seymour conjecture (about induced wheels in triangle-free Burling graphs) but does not address the 4-hole controlled question. No follow-up resolving the ℓ = 4 case was found in any searched source.
